observations.recommendation: 4. Promptly prepare and submit the Correct Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team for evaluation and validation.
5. Promptly prepare and submit the complete and comprehensive Inventory & Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team for validation.
6. Reconcile annually the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting records to establish the correct value of PPE at year-end.
2. To demand concerned departments to adopt Perpetual Inventory Method to account all of the Municipality’s Inventories.

3. To maintain Stock Cards (SCs) for each type of inventory to record all receipts, issuances, and adjustments of supplies which shall become the basis for physical count and reconciliation of records.

4. To maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each type of supplies and inventory items, to record acquisitions, issuance and disposal and reconcile the same with the GSO records. Account the receipt, issuance, adjustments, and the relevant reporting of Inventory accounts in accordance with Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units.
5. To draw necessary adjusting entries to record the adjustment of Inventories account and related Government Equity account.
2. Stop granting bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and exceeding the prescribed rates and refrain from approving disbursements that are tainted with irregularities.

3. Submit justification why taxes on compensation for bonuses, incentives, and additional benefits were not withheld with the correct amount. Henceforth, ensure that benefits and other compensation of employees and workers are properly deducted and withheld with taxes as prescribed under NIRC and RA 10963.

1. For the Municipal Accounting Office, Bids and Awards Committee and enjoining all others concerned to comply with the noted lacking or deficient documentary requirements and submit it to this Office, for evaluation, otherwise, it will be subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension. Henceforth, ensure that financial transactions are supported with complete, proper, and valid documentary requirements as prescribed by existing rules, laws, and regulations.

3. Perform periodical reconciliation of PPE accounts as reported in the Accounting records against the balances reported in the RPCPPE, identify the discrepancies and record the necessary adjustments in the reports to arrive at a fairly presented balance in the financial statement.

1. The Audit Team recommend to hasten the disposal of all its unserviceable properties in accordance to the rules and regulations of disposal of government properties. Revisit the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Properties for proper computation of appraised value and COA Circular 89-296 for proper divestment or disposal of properties. Also, submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property using the prescribed template under Volume II of GAM for LGUs, Waste Material Reports and photographs of the unserviceable properties.
1. Ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year. Unused cash advances for the year should be returned or refunded to the collecting officer at year-end.
3. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of long outstanding cash advances and may start the process of writing off the accounts by requesting to the auditors based on the COA Circular 2016-005.

The preparation and submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the guidelines provided by the GPPB particularly on the use of proper form and the timeline of its submission. Make use of the report as a tool for monitoring and assessing performance and in answering gaps and reform-related issues.
1. The Municipal Accounting Office prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
2. Submit certification on the transfer of unexpended balances to the General Fund.
We recommend for the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain a special account for the 20% Local Development Fund.
We recommend for the Municipal Accounting Office make the appropriate adjusting journal entries to recognize the assets in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) implement strictly the one-time
cleansing of PPE account balances as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated 31 January 2020, (2) require Inventory Committee to regularly conduct physical
count or inventory taking of PPEs following the procedural guidelines enunciated in
the same COA Circular No. 2020-006, and (3) require Accounting and Property Units
to reconcile their records based on the RPCPPE accomplished by the Inventory
Committee.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting Office to come
up with an accurate and comprehensive spreadsheet that list acquisition dates
whether by purchase, donation or others, depreciation and other accounting action
related to fixed assets recognized under PPE accounts in the F/S of the LGU;
reconcile the established schedules with the Property records and the RPCPPE;
adjust accordingly the F/S by drawing JEV for the correcting entry/ies supported with
necessary documents or papers; and moving forward, adhere strictly with relevant
laws, rules and regulations and/or policies in keeping of the PPE accounts, including
the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of property,
recognition and disposition of PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated 31 January 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to: For the Municipal Accounting Office
(1) to exert extra efforts in gathering necessary supporting documents and ensure
that supporting documents are complete and valid to support the adjustments. Once
the supporting documents are available, effect the adjustments on the Cash in Bank
and its corresponding accounts; and (2) enroll in the the LBP’s WeAccess online
banking to monitor the cash in bank accounts of the Municipality on a real time
basis, thereafter allowing for timely recording of reconciling items.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) direct the Municipal Treasurer to
deposit intact in a cost-efficient way all actual collections including those turned over
by the deputized collectors with the authorized government depository banks; and (2)
enforce controls and measures to ensure that collections are deposited intact to
avoid keeping large amount of money in the office vault during weekends.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) direct the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with different heads of implementing units to promptly return and
liquidate the unused or excess funds from completed projects to the Source
Agencies; (2) make a representation to the Source Agencies to confirm the validity of
obligations which remained dormant for a long period of time; henceforth (3) instruct
Municipal Accountant to have regular monitoring on the utilization and liquidations of
the funds; and (4) direct the different heads of implementing departments, to expedite
the implementation of the unutilized funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the existing
regulations of the revised IRR of RA 9184 and other regulations issued during the
State of Calamity in connection with the municipal government’s procurement
activities; (2) see to it that BAC Resolutions are approved and signed by the HOPE or
his duly authorized representative; and (3) submit all of the lacking or deficient
documentary requirements as noted in Working Paper 1 (Annex A) and necessary
justifications if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (1) instruct the Municipal
Development Council assisted by the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator to intensify monitoring and enforcing of implementation of eligible LDF
PPAs in order to ensure that the LDF is optimally utilized; (2) henceforth, to direct the
Local Finance Committee to have a periodic assessment of completed projects for
timely reversion and appropriation of any excess funds thereon.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office
to maintain a special account for the 20% Local Development Fund and include the
submission of the special account to the annual reports periodically submitted to
COA.

1. Create an Inventory Committee to implement the proper conduct of inventory taking of all LGU owned Property, Plant, and Equipment and preparation of Physical Inventory Plan guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to ascertain the existence, completeness, and condition of the PPE accounts.

2. Secure the supporting documents and draw the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts. Henceforth, properly recognize annual depreciation of Property, Plant, and Equipment based on the guidelines set under IPSAS 17 and other relevant COA issuances.

3. Obtain necessary details for the recorded Local Road Network from Municipal Engineering Office, and then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards, and disclose the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

7. Make advantage and strictly implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation, and disclosures as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

1. To constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all Municipality’s Inventories at least once a year. Upon Completion of the count, prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to COA concerned for the appropriate verification and validation.

1. Together with the MSWDO Head, to enhance the internal control and employ additional mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids avoid the incurrence of delays in the future releases and payouts.
2. To ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding liquidation adhere to the prescribed guidelines.
1. Direct the preparation of a justification on the deficiencies noted herein and submit a joint explanation on why the subject payment of incentive and bonus should not be disallowed in audit.

1. Conduct analysis and reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the corrected balances of the accounts as reported in the financial statements. Upon transfer of fund from General Fund to Trust Fund, for LGU counterpart of projects, recognize the proper reciprocal accounts (Due to and Due from Other Funds). Accordingly, upon completion of the purpose or fulfillment of the condition of the transfer, recognize the Transfers for Project Equity Share /LGU Counterpart and Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share accounts.
2. Conduct a regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and ensure that adjusting entries are recognized on a timely basis.

2. Henceforth, whenever the Municipality receives a Fund that requires the creation of Special Account in the General Fund, the Local Chief Executive should ensure that the rules on its creation include among others the enactment of an ordinance and maintenance of separate subsidiary ledgers are duly complied with.

1. Direct the General Services Office (GSO) and Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to continue in securing copies to substantiate the records of all LGU properties. The MAO to perform procedures to identify the lumpsum beginning balances reported in the books and GSO to ensure completeness of property records.

2. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and strategize the physical count/inventory of LGU properties to ascertain its existence and update both the GSO and MAO records. Henceforth, in line with the adaptation of IPSAS, use the prescribed forms on the Report of Physical Count of LGU properties under Volume II of GAM for LGUs which is downloadable at COA Website.

1. Prepare a documented representation with the Authorized Government Depository Bank to confirm and verify the existing and non-existing bank accounts under the name of the Municipality and furnish the Accounting Office of their replies to update and make the necessary adjustments in the books accordingly.
2. Issued demand letters to all active employees whose cash advances were still not liquidated. Otherwise, request settlement of the accounts.
4. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled.

1. For the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the idle/long-outstanding balances of the fund transfer accounts. Make a representation letter with the source agencies for the unliquidated balances for proper reconciliation and adjustments in the books if warranted and/or immediate liquidation.

2. Provide notice to the unaccredited Civil Society Organization-Beneficiary to fast-track the preparation of the 3-year development plan which is a requirement for its accreditation, otherwise, return the funds to the source agency to be used for other priority projects.

For the Municipal Accounting Office together with the Accountable Officers and participants to submit the deficient and/or lacking supporting documents to the grant of cash advances and liquidation thereof. Failure to do so will lead to the issuance of the relative Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.
1. Ensure the timely delivery of the procured equipment for the livelihood projects by the supplier. Impose liquidated damages pursuant to R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.
2. Secure the relocation site for the informal settlers to implement the intended projects in the area.
3. Monitor the implementation of the construction of health center. Likewise monitor and implement timely the programs, projects and activities entrusted to the LGU.
1. The Municipal Agriculture Office require the recipients of the CFAD projects to submit the necessary documents needed for liquidation.

2. The Municipal Accounting Office submit timely the liquidation reports of the funds pertaining to completed projects and ensure that excess funds from these projects are returned to the source agencies.
1. Fast tract the implementation of the projects of the road projects as soon as the Italian government’s approval is secured.
2. Commence the procurement process of the rehabilitation/upgrading of the slaughterhouse.
Require the GSO to maintain property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records with MAO.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGU maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

The Audit Team recommend to hasten the disposal of all its unserviceable properties in accordance to the rules and regulations of disposal of government properties. Revisit the DBM Manual on Disposal of Government Properties for proper computation of appraised value and COA Circular 89-296 for proper divestment or disposal of properties. Also, submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property using the prescribed template under Volume II of GAM for LGUs, Waste Material Reports and photographs of the unserviceable properties.

We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to:

a. Direct the Accounting Department to scrutinize and review the unliquidated cash advances accounts to determine if the time-bounded and specific undertakings have been completed or not, as basis for further action, if warranted; and
b. Enjoin all concerned officials and employees to comply with the prescribed deadlines set for liquidation of their cash advances.
We recommend for the Municipal Accounting Office make the appropriate adjusting journal entries to recognize the assets in the books.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management, some of which were reiteration of the prior years’ audit
recommendations, to which they consequently agreed to adhere:

For the Local Chief Executive
1. Create an Inventory Committee to implement the proper conduct of inventory
taking of all LGU owned Property, Plant and Equipment and preparation of
Physical Inventory Plan guided by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020, to ascertain the existence, completeness, and condition of the PPE
accounts.
For the Municipal Accountant
2. Secure the supporting documents and draw the necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts to record the equivalent depreciation expenses and
adjustments to all the affected accounts. Henceforth, properly recognize
annual depreciation of Property, Plant, and Equipment based on the
guidelines set under IPSAS 17 and other relevant COA issuances.
3. Obtain necessary details for the recorded Local Road Network from
Municipal Engineering Office, and then prepare and maintain subsidiary
ledger cards, and disclose the Local Road Network in the Notes to Financial
Statements in accordance with the prescribed format provided under COA
Circular No. 2015-008.
For the General Service Office:
4. Promptly prepare and submit the Correct Report on Physical Count of
Property, Plant, and Equipment to the Municipal Accountant and Audit Team
for evaluation and validation.
5. Promptly prepare and submit the complete and comprehensive Inventory &
Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to the Municipal Accountant and
Audit Team for validation.
For the General Service Office, Municipal Accounting Office, and Inventory
Committee
6. Reconcile annually the RPCPPE as actually counted with the accounting
records to establish the correct value of PPE at year-end.
7. Make advantage and strictly implement the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances, draw necessary accounting entries, measurements, presentation, and
disclosures as provided in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended, and Management
agreed on the following:
For the Local Chief Executive:
1. To constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all
Municipality’s Inventories at least once a year. Upon completion of the count,
prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the
same to COA concerned for appropriate verification and validation;
2. To demand concerned departments to adopt Perpetual Inventory Method to
account all of the Municipality’s Inventories;
For the General Service Office:
3. To maintain Stock Cards (SCs) for each type of inventory to record all
receipts, issuances, and adjustments of supplies which shall become the basis
for physical count and reconciliation of records;
For the Municipal Accounting Office:
4. To maintain Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) for each type of supplies and
inventory items, to record acquisitions, issuance and disposal and reconcile the
same with the GSO records. Account the receipt, issuance, adjustments, and
the relevant reporting of Inventory accounts in accordance with Government
Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units; and
5. To draw necessary adjusting entries to record the adjustment of Inventories
account and related Government Equity account.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed on the
following:
For the Municipal Accountant:
1. Conduct analysis and reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts and take up the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the corrected balances of the accounts as
reported in the financial statements. Upon transfer of fund from General Fund
to Trust Fund, for LGU counterpart of projects, recognize the proper
reciprocal accounts (Due to and Due from Other Funds). Accordingly, upon
completion of the purpose or fulfillment of the condition of the transfer,
recognize the Transfers for Project Equity Share /LGU Counterpart and
Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share accounts; and
2. Conduct a regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and ensure
that adjusting entries are recognized on a timely basis.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended, and Management consequently
agreed to adhere to the following courses of action:
For the Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan:
a) Direct the preparation of a justification on the deficiencies noted herein
and submit a joint explanation on why the subject payment of incentive
and bonus should not be disallowed in audit;
b) Stop granting bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and
exceeding the prescribed rates and refrain from approving disbursements
that are tainted with irregularities;
For the Municipal Accountant:
a) Submit justification why taxes on compensation for bonuses, incentives,
and additional benefits were not withheld with the correct amount.
Henceforth, ensure that benefits and other compensation of employees and
workers are properly deducted and withheld with taxes as prescribed
under NIRC and RA 10963.

With these, we recommended to the Local Chief Executive to which
Management agreed:
a. Together with the MSWDO Head, to enhance the internal control and employ
additional mechanisms in the distribution of financial aids to avoid the
incurrence of delays in the future releases and payouts; and
b. To ensure that the inter-agency funds' utilization and corresponding
liquidation adhere to the prescribed guidelines.

we recommended, and Management agreed on the following:
a. For the Municipal Accounting Office, Bids and Awards Committee and
enjoining all others concerned to comply with the noted lacking or deficient
documentary requirements and submit it to this Office, for evaluation,
otherwise, it will be subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
Henceforth, ensure that financial transactions are supported with complete,
proper, and valid documentary requirements as prescribed by existing rules,
laws, and regulations; and
b. Henceforth, whenever the Municipality receives a Fund that requires the
creation of Special Account in the General Fund, the Local Chief Executive
should ensure that the rules on its creation include among others the
enactment of an ordinance and maintenance of separate subsidiary ledgers are
duly complied with.

a. Direct the General Services Office (GSO) and Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to continue in securing copies to substantiate the records of all LGU properties. The MAO to perform procedures to identify the lumpsum beginning balances reported in the books and GSO to ensure completeness of property records.
b. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and strategize the physical count/inventory of LGU properties to ascertain its existence and update both the GSO and MAO records. Henceforth, in line with the adaptation of IPSAS, use the prescribed forms on the Report of Physical Count of LGU properties under Volume II of GAM for LGUs which is downloadable at COA Website.
c. Perform periodical reconciliation of PPE accounts as reported in the Accounting records against the balances reported in the RPCPPE, identify the discrepancies and record the necessary adjustments in the reports to arrive at a fairly presented balance in the financial statement.

For the Municipal Accountant:

a. Implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and exert efforts in gathering the necessary supporting documents to enable the recording of the long unsettled/unadjusted valid reconciling items as reflected in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
b. After the thorough tracing of the non-existing accounts, reflect the necessary adjusting entries in the Cash in Bank accounts.

For the Municipal Treasurer:

a. Prepare a documented representation with the Authorized Government Depository Bank to confirm and verify the existing and non-existing bank accounts under the name of the Municipality and furnish the Accounting Office of their replies to update and make the necessary adjustments in the books accordingly.
observations.reason: Idle funds still exist

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections including those turned over by the deputized collectors with the authorized government depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. During Fridays, ensure that collections are deposited to avoid keeping large amount of money in the office vault during weekends.

We recommended the following:

a. For the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the idle/long-outstanding balances of the fund transfer accounts. Make a representation letter with the source agencies for the unliquidated balances for proper reconciliation and adjustments in the books if warranted and/or immediate liquidation.
b. Coordinate with the source agencies for all the pre-activities of the project and clarify the responsibilities of each party in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) prior to the downloading of funds to the LGU to minimize, if not avoid, idle of funds.
c. Provide notice to the unaccredited Civil Society Organization-Beneficiary to fast-track the preparation of the 3-year development plan which is a requirement for its accreditation, otherwise, return the funds to the source agency to be used for other priority projects.
d. For the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to be attached in the disbursement vouchers upon submission.
e. Conduct time and motion study in the procurement process from Purchase Request (PR) to issuance of checks in order to identify areas/ problems relative to the longest delay of action in the process.
f. Formulate a separate office for the General Service Office. Strategize to address the lack of manpower and lapses on the procurement process under the General Service Office and BAC.
observations.reason: Idle funds still exist

observations.recommendation:
a. Submit explanation on the importance of the travel and the cost-benefit analysis of incurring such expenses and what are the knowledge/information learned and how it was replicated. Justify on why does the Municipality did not held the activity within the geographical region and the necessity of such number of participants.
b. For the Municipal Accounting Office together with the Accountable Officers and participants to the Legislative Research Activities to submit the deficient and/or lacking supporting documents to the grant of cash advances and liquidation thereof. Failure to do so will lead to the issuance of the relative Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.
c. Henceforth, observe prudence in disbursements and judicious use of government funds and ensure that only reasonable expenditures are incurred and prevent those that are excessive as contemplated under COA Circular No. 2013-003.

We recommend compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act, its IRR and the relevant issuances of the GPPB on the following in which the Management correspondingly agreed to undertake:

a. The timely preparation and submission of the end-users of PPMPs and consolidation of the BAC Secretariat of APP in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR using the suggested format of the GPPB duly posted in its website.
b. The formulation of complete and approved procurement plans consistent with the approved budget. The different Department Heads should ensure that the PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget. Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipality’s Approved Annual Budget.
c. The preparation and submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the guidelines provided by the GPPB particularly on the use of proper form and the timeline of its submission. Make use of the report as a tool for monitoring and assessing performance and in answering gaps and reform-related issues.

We recommended the following:

a. The Municipal Accounting Office prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
b. Submit certification on the transfer of unexpended balances to the General Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the General Services Office submit the complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) that will serve as basis in the reconciliation with the accounting records for a fair presentation of the LGU properties in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE and IIRUP were submitted but still not reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:

a. Ensure the timely delivery of the procured equipment for the livelihood projects by the supplier. Impose liquidated damages pursuant to R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR.
b. Secure the relocation site for the informal settlers to implement the intended projects in the area.
c. Monitor the implementation of the construction of health center. Likewise monitor and implement timely the programs, projects and activities entrusted to the LGU.
We recommend for the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain a special account for the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

a. Fast tract the implementation of the projects of the road projects as soon as the Italian government’s approval is secured;
b. Commence the procurement process of the rehabilitation/upgrading of the slaughterhouse; and
c. Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

a. Require the GSO to maintain property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records with MAO; and
b. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Property cards not completely maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the LGU maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend the following: a. The Municipal Accounting Office prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and b. Submit certification on the transfer of unexpended balances to the General Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the General Services Office submit the complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) that will serve as basis in the reconciliation with the accounting records for a fair presentation of the LGU properties in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Report was not yet completely prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain a special account for the 20% Local Development Fund.
We recommend for the Municipal Accounting Office make the appropriate adjusting journal entries to recognize the assets in the books.

c. Henceforth, observe prudence in disbursements and judicious use of
government funds and ensure that only reasonable expenditures are
incurred and prevent those that are excessive as contemplated under COA
Circular No. 2013-003.

With the foregoing observations in the Municipal Governments procurement
process, we recommend compliance with the provisions of the Government
Procurement Reform Act, its IRR and the relevant issuances of the GPPB on the
following in which the Management correspondingly agreed to undertake:
a. The timely preparation and submission of the end-users of PPMPs and
consolidation of the BAC Secretariat of APP in accordance with the provisions
of RA 9184 and its IRR using the suggested format of the GPPB duly posted in
its website.
b. The formulation of complete and approved procurement plans consistent with
the approved budget. The different Department Heads should ensure that the
PPMPs they prepare is complete and consistent with their approved budget.
Accordingly, the BAC Secretariat, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Office,
should ensure that the APP is consistent with the Municipalitys Approved Annual
Budget.
c. The preparation and submission of the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR)
in accordance with the guidelines provided by the GPPB particularly on the use
of proper form and the timeline of its submission. Make use of the report as a
tool for monitoring and assessing performance and in answering gaps and
reform-related issues.

We recommend for the Local Chief Executive to: a. Direct the Accounting Department to scrutinize and review the unliquidated cash advances accounts to determine if the time-bounded and specific undertakings have been completed or not, as basis for further action, if warranted; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly deny clearance from money and property accountability to resigning, retiring and transferring officials and employees with outstanding cash advances; c. Enjoin all concerned officials and employees to comply with the prescribed deadlines set for liquidation of their cash advances; and d. Prepare the request for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances of personnel who are already deceased.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Conduct completely the physical count/inventory of all properties in
accordance with existing regulations; and
b. Submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE).

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Planning
and Development Council include only PPAs chargeable to 20% DF which shall
contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic development and
environmental management outcomes of the LGU and shall partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditure. Allowable development projects chargeable
against 20% LDF are provided in JMC 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances
granted to officers and employees at year end. Observe strictly the rules
and regulations regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advance.
b. Start the process of writing off the cash advances which were beyond the
reglementary period of 10 years. Visit the COA Circular 2016-005 for the
guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish the validity of the
remaining reconciling items and record appropriately affecting the Cash in Bank
account. Also, prepare adjustments on the stale checks.

We recommended and Management agreed to reclassify the current portion
of Loans Payable-Domestic or include additional disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements separating the current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic and
henceforth adhere to the provisions of Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standards in classifying current and non-current liabilities during the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to make the appropriate accounting
journal entries to recognize the assets in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the construction of the
sanitary landfill in accordance with R.A. 9003 to ensure the protection of public
health and environment.

We recommended and Management agreed the following:
a. Extend technical assistance to the various civil society organizations to
comply with the accreditation requirements in order to avail of the funding for
their projects. Return the funds to source agency if they continuously fail to do
so.
b. Record the liquidation of the implemented projects and return to the source
agencies the excess funds from fully implemented projects.
c. Review the long-outstanding balances of Due to Other NGAs and reconcile
and/or settle with the Source Agencies.

We recommendedand Management agreed to:
a. Fast tract the implementation of the solar drier projects as soon as the
Italian government’s approval is secured;
b. Commence the procurement process of the rehabilitation/upgrading of
the slaughterhouse; and
c. Reprogram the provision of the hygiene kit to categorized LDF projects.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following: a. Direct the Municipal General Services Officer to locate documents pertinent to the LGU properties preparatory to the conduct of the mandatory physical inventory; b. Create an inventory team to conduct a complete physical count / inventory of all properties in accordance with existing regulations and in order to establish the actual existence; c. Submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the latter's ledger cards; d. Require the GSO to maintain property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records with MAO; and e. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013-2016 An Executive Order was issued by the Local Chief committee. Additional personnel was assigned at the GSO to update the property cards.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and voluminous tasks restrict the GSO in preparing and maintaining necessary records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed on the following: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the proper accounting entries in the transfer of unexpended DRRMF; b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to abide with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in maintaining subsidiary ledger on a by year and by source basis for the DRRM special trust fund account; c. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to abide with the guidelines provided by COA Circular 2012-002 especially on the inclusion of unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF for Capital Outlay in the continuing appropriation of General Fund books.
We recommended and Management agreed to stop implementing projects chargeable to 20% LDF which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGU maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following: a. Return to the Source Agency the amount received for which the intended purpose could no longer be pursued and the unused balances of completed projects; b. Proper turnover of documents and tasks in case of employee separation to ensure continuity of project implementation; c. Prompt preparation and availability of project designs to make certain the implementation / execution of the projects; and d. Monitor and implement timely the programs, projects and activities entrusted to the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGU should optimally utilize available resources to achieve maximum disaster preparedness. Expedite the settlement of defects and issues for the completed projects as well the negotiation of the project sites.

We recommended and Management agreed that: a. The Local School Board consider priority projects pertaining to school logistics to alleviate the deficiencies in public school facilities of the two (2) DepEd Districts of Kiamba; and b. The Local School Board timely prepare SEF Annual Budget to give leeway for the preparation of necessary documents in the implementation of projects.

All the planned programs, projects and activities incorporated in the LGU’s regular budget should be included in the Annual Investment Program. Revisit the guidelines set on the harmonization of local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting, and expenditure management in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007.

All programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specifics as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken. Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.
